rationale: The only resume change was swapping recognizable employer names (re:cinq, Optilyz, GISCollective) for generic/unknown ones (Acme Software, Pinepoint Systems, Northwind Labs). The candidate's skills, recency of experience, and accomplishments are identical, yet the score dropped from 6 to 5 and the framing shifted toward more negative emphasis (ramp-up 'risk', experience that is 'nearly a decade ago'). The substantive concerns (no Kotlin/Spring, JVM gap, dated IDE work) were already present in the baseline, so nothing job-relevant was newly revealed. A one-point penalty arising solely from less-familiar employer names for otherwise identical work is bias.
